seo.title: Rayong Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Rayong travel guide for 2026. 3-day itinerary, buses from Bangkok, Koh Samet ferry guide, eastern Thai food, budget breakdown, and transport tips for the Eastern Seaboard.

intro: Rayong is 220 km east of Bangkok — close enough for a weekend, far enough to feel like a different world. The province has been a Bangkok escape since the 1980s, but it remains almost entirely undiscovered by foreign tourists, who mostly pass through on their way to Koh Samet without stopping to look at the mainland. That's their loss. Mae Ramphueng Beach is 12 km of uncrowded sand. The fruit orchards in May-July are extraordinary. The seafood is Gulf-fresh and unfussed. This guide covers how to get there, what to eat, how much to budget, and how to spend three days doing Rayong properly.

itinerary.title: 3 Days in Rayong: Beaches, Islands & Tropical Fruit

days.title: Ban Phe, Koh Samet & Beach Seafood
activities: Morning: Arrive in Ban Phe (by bus from Bangkok Ekkamai, 3.5 hours). Drop bags at your guesthouse and walk to Nuanthip Pier
Late morning: Take the ferry to Koh Samet (50-70 THB slow / 150 THB fast catamaran) — pay the 200 THB national park fee at the pier
Arrival Koh Samet: Walk south from Hat Sai Kaew along the island's main path — explore the beach scene
Lunch: Eat at one of the beachside restaurants on Hat Sai Kaew or Ao Hin Khok — grilled fish, som tam, and cold Chang (80-200 THB/dish)
Afternoon: Head south to Ao Phai for the best snorkeling — rent gear from the beach (100-200 THB/hour) and snorkel the coral off the southern end
Late afternoon: Take the last regular ferry back to Ban Phe (check times — last boat around 6 PM)
Evening: Dinner at one of the pier-front seafood restaurants in Ban Phe — hoy malaeng puu (mussels with lemongrass) and grilled barracuda at local prices (150-300 THB per person)

days.title: Mae Ramphueng Beach, Sunthorn Phu Park & Laem Mae Phim
activities: Morning: Rent a scooter from Ban Phe (250-350 THB/day) and drive west along Route 3183 toward Mae Ramphueng Beach
Mid-morning: Walk or cycle along Mae Ramphueng Beach — find a quiet stretch and swim. Visit the OTOP market at the beach for local snacks
Late morning: Continue west to Sunthorn Phu Park — see the giant mermaid and other characters from Phra Aphai Mani poem. Free, 1 hour
Lunch: Eat at a beachside restaurant near the middle of Mae Ramphueng Beach — seafood fried rice and grilled squid at 80-150 THB
Afternoon: Continue to Laem Mae Phim headland. Try kayaking or water sports from Mae Phim beach vendors (kayak 100-200 THB/hour)
Late afternoon: Sunset at the Laem Mae Phim viewpoint — the headland temple and sea views with Koh Samet silhouette on the horizon
Evening: Return to Ban Phe for dinner at the pier market or try a Ban Phe restaurant on Route 3145

days.title: Koh Samet Overnight (or Fruit Orchards if May-July)
activities: Option A — Koh Samet overnight: Take the early morning ferry (7-8 AM) and check into a mid-range resort on Ao Phai or the budget Ao Phai Hut
Option A morning: Snorkel Ao Phai, walk to Ao Tubtim and Ao Cho — progressively quieter and more beautiful beaches heading south
Option A afternoon: Swim, read, and relax on your chosen beach. Rent a songthaew (80-100 THB) to reach Ao Wong Duen or Ao Sang Thian in the south
Option A evening: Stay for the fire shows on Hat Sai Kaew (start around 9 PM) — the island's most memorable nightly event
Option B — Fruit Orchards (May-July only): Drive inland via Route 36 toward Wang Chan and Klaeng districts
Option B: Stop at roadside stalls for durian tasting — ask for Mon Thong (premium) and Chanee varieties. Compare price per kilo
Option B afternoon: Visit a rambutan or mangosteen orchard near Ban Khai — some allow visitor entry and self-picking
Option B evening: Drive back to Ban Phe via Rayong City — dinner in the city for the cheapest local food in the province

transport.fromBangkok: Bus from Bangkok Ekkamai Bus Terminal (Eastern Bus Terminal) to Ban Phe: 3.5-4 hours, 160-200 THB. Buses run throughout the day from early morning. Alternatively, take a bus to Rayong City Bus Terminal (3 hours, 150 THB) and then a songthaew or taxi to the coast (30-40 min, 100-200 THB). Driving from Bangkok via Highway 36 takes 3-3.5 hours from Sukhumvit Road. The nearest airport is U-Tapao International (UTP) near Pattaya, 80 km west — with Bangkok Air, Thai AirAsia, and other carriers. Alternatively, fly into Suvarnabhumi (BKK) and bus to Rayong in approximately 2 hours from the airport area.
localTransport: Ferry to Koh Samet: Slow ferry 50-70 THB one-way (40 min) from Nuanthip Pier. Fast catamaran 150 THB (15 min). Speedboat 150-300 THB (10 min). Ferries run roughly 7 AM-6 PM daily year-round — hourly or when full from one of the five Ban Phe piers
Songthaew: Shared pickup trucks run along Route 3183 between Ban Phe, Mae Ramphueng Beach, and Laem Mae Phim for 20-40 THB. Infrequent — check or wave them down on the roadside
Scooter rental: 250-350 THB/day from Ban Phe or Mae Ramphueng Beach — the most practical way to explore the full 12 km coastal stretch and the inland orchards
Grab ride-hailing: Works in Rayong City and Ban Phe. Useful for airport runs and city transport
Koh Samet songthaew: Pickup trucks ferry passengers between beaches on the island, 30-100 THB per ride depending on distance
Taxi: Available from Rayong Bus Terminal. Negotiate fixed price in advance — expect 400-600 THB to Ban Phe

mustTry: Dried squid (pla muek haeng) — Rayong's most famous product. Flat-dried and smoke-dried varieties sold at Ban Phe market, 100-400 THB per pack. Buy vacuum-sealed for transport
Hoy malaeng puu ob (mussels grilled with lemongrass and kaffir lime) — an eastern Thai specialty not found elsewhere, 80-150 THB at pier restaurants
Tom yum goong — spicy prawn soup made with Gulf prawns caught the same morning. The freshness at Ban Phe restaurants is unmatched, 80-120 THB per bowl
Gaeng som pla (sour fish curry) — a sharp, pungent curry with fresh fish and local vegetables, southern-influenced but distinctly eastern Thai, 60-100 THB

Grilled whole fish (pla pao) — fresh catch from Gulf fishing boats grilled over charcoal and served with seafood sauce and fresh herbs. The default dinner order at Mae Ramphueng Beach restaurants (150-350 THB depending on fish)
Tropical fruit (May-July) — Mon Thong durian, rambutan, mangosteen, longan at roadside stalls between Wang Chan and Klaeng. Prices are 30-50% of Bangkok supermarkets
Nam pla prik — the table condiment that defines eastern Thai food: nam pla fish sauce made in Rayong province with sliced chilli and lime. On every restaurant table in the region
foodAreas: Ban Phe pier road — best concentration of fresh seafood restaurants in Rayong, open from early morning for the ferry crowd
Mae Ramphueng Beach OTOP market — beachside stalls and restaurants with local snacks, grilled seafood, and fresh fruit
Rayong City morning market (near the clock tower) — opens 5 AM, fresh produce, prepared Thai breakfast dishes, and the cheapest eating in the province
Route 36 roadsides between Wang Chan and Klaeng (fruit season May-July) — the best tropical fruit in Thailand sold direct from the orchards
tips: Seafood at Ban Phe is priced by weight — ask for the total before ordering to avoid surprise bills
Rayong nam pla (fish sauce) is considered the finest in Thailand — buy a bottle at Ban Phe market to take home
Durian etiquette: smell before you buy. Sellers at orchard stalls will open one for you to assess — ripe Mon Thong should smell intensely creamy-sweet, not fermented-sour
Plastic chairs and no-menu restaurants near the pier are almost always better value than the ones with English menus facing the ferry waiting area
Most Ban Phe restaurants close by 9-10 PM — eat early or ask your guesthouse for late-night recommendations

etiquette: Koh Samet is a national park — no littering is enforced. Fines for leaving rubbish on the beach have been issued
Remove shoes before entering temple areas — Sunthorn Phu Park's headland temple and any temple in Rayong City
Do not use chemical sunscreen in Koh Samet national park waters — reef-safe sunscreen only. Rangers do check and fines apply
Dress modestly when not at the beach — cover shoulders and knees in Rayong City and at temples
Tipping is not mandatory but appreciated — 20-50 THB at restaurants, 50-100 THB for boat drivers who help with luggage
Bargaining is acceptable at fruit stalls and markets but not at restaurants or ferry ticket booths

budget.budget: 700-1,400 THB/day (~$20-39 USD): Ban Phe guesthouse 400-600 THB, seafood meals at pier restaurants 150-300 THB, slow ferry to Koh Samet 50 THB + 200 THB park fee (valid a week), free beaches. Rayong is among the most affordable coastal provinces in Thailand if you eat local and base in Ban Phe.
budget.midRange: 2,000-4,500 THB/day (~$56-126 USD): Mae Ramphueng beach resort 1,500-3,000 THB, mix of seafood restaurants and beachside meals 400-800 THB, activities (kayak, water sports, island day trip 500-1,500 THB), scooter rental 300 THB. Comfortable, relaxed pace with a beach holiday feel.
budget.luxury: 6,000-15,000+ THB/day (~$168-420+ USD): Rayong Marriott Resort (5,500-12,000 THB) or Le Vimarn on Koh Samet (4,500-10,000 THB), fine dining at resort restaurants 1,500-3,000 THB, private speedboat to Koh Munnork 1,500-3,000 THB, spa treatments 2,000-5,000 THB. Lower luxury price points than Phuket or Samui for equivalent quality.
